published_prior_art.cite: BaitairianSebastienJreichKatsahianGuilloux2025 + FanGuerreZhu2017/FirpoRidder2019: sharp APO and joint-distribution/coupling bounds, but not this IV-compatible adjacent-rank LP target

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_dose_response_shape_v1 has a shape-induced three-dose phase transition, not a copula-radius threshold for adjacent-rank slopes

published_prior_art.cite: FirpoRidder2019 and FanGuerreZhu2017 motivate non-joint sharpness and copula information, but do not give a sensitivity-margin/copula-radius derivative-sign boundary

repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md §3 empty PartialID catalogue; no in-catalogue no-tube rank-slice reduction

published_prior_art.cite: JessonDouglasManshausenSolalMeinshausenStierGalShalit2022, MarmarelisHaddadJessonJahanshadGalstyanVerSteeg2023, and BaitairianSebastienJreichKatsahianGuilloux2025 cover dosewise continuous-sensitivity envelopes; this is a sanity reduction of the new target

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add an explicit two-by-two numerical witness template with row/column margins, cell slopes, IV moment inequality, and the resulting lower-bound calculation so Conjecture 1 can be attacked directly in Stage 0.
Sharpen Conjecture 2 by stating the critical radius as an LP active-constraint threshold or a minimum over finitely many affine inequalities in the two-bin witness.

Add one sentence in §5 noting that Zhang2025ADE is an average-over-exposure derivative estimand and therefore does not condition on U_a0 in tau; this keeps the closest-comparator distinction auditable.

recommended_next_step: Proceed to Stage 0; prioritize the finite LP theorem and an explicit two-bin witness enumeration for the sign certificate.
